critic_raw:
Issue 1: Temporal confound — tax-refund season. Feb–Apr systematically increases voluntary repayment. The before-period lacks this liquidity boost. The model gets unearned credit.

Issue 2: Simultaneous dialer change. New vendor improved phone-number verification, changing the queue composition by introducing previously-unreachable accounts. Higher recovered balance per assigned account follows directly from better contactability.

Issue 3: Manager override contamination. Model rank is not the sole determinant of who gets contacted. Discretionary front-loading of hardship/promised-to-pay accounts introduces uncontrolled manager judgment into treatment assignment.

Issue 4: No same-period control. Before-after without concurrent control cannot isolate model contribution from the combination of seasonal, dialer, and override effects.
defender_raw: Issues 1–4 are valid methodological concerns. The memo's claim is too strong. The appropriate framing is: the combined new system produced a 16% recovery improvement in an evaluation period that was not controlled for confounds. A champion-challenger test is warranted before attributing the gain to the model specifically.

debate_rounds.critic: The defender has conceded all four issues. No substantive disagreement remains about the methodological validity of the causal claim. The only remaining question is how to proceed: I recommend a randomized champion-challenger design where accounts in the same Feb–Apr window are randomly assigned to model-ranked or legacy-ranked queues with the same dialer and documented override policy. This design addresses all four issues simultaneously.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed on the champion-challenger design. I would add that the test should also track override rates by condition to ensure the manager-judgment confounder is visible. Accept the proposed empirical test.

adjudication_raw: Immediate convergence after round 1 — defender conceded all four issues in the initial response and accepted the empirical test proposal. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed. All issues resolved by concession and mutual acceptance of the empirical test design.

empirical_test.condition: Randomized champion-challenger within the same calendar period: concurrent model-ranked versus legacy-ranked queues with same dialer, tracked override rates
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No material recovery difference between conditions in a concurrent test
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Model-ranked condition shows materially higher recovery per account in the concurrent test
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Model shows positive signal but smaller than reported board figure, with residual gap unexplained by observed covariates
